extra.title: Non-optional return type masks optionality of underlying API
extra.body: The method returns `String` but `UserDefaults.standard.string(forKey:)` returns `String?`. Force-unwrapping hides the fact that the value may be nil. This creates a misleading public API signature—callers will assume a username always exists when it may not, leading to runtime crashes.
extra.suggested_action: Change return type to `String?` to accurately reflect that a username may not be present in UserDefaults.

extra.title: New public method `fetchUser` signature changed without test coverage
extra.body: The `fetchUser` method's return type changed from `User?` to `User` (non-optional), which is effectively a new public API contract. No test coverage for this modified entry point is visible in the diff. The change also introduces force-unwrapping logic that should be verified through tests.
extra.suggested_action: Add test cases covering `fetchUser` to verify the non-optional return behavior and error handling.

extra.title: New public method `currentUserName` added without test coverage
extra.body: A new public method `currentUserName()` was added but no corresponding test case appears in the diff. This method reads from UserDefaults and force-unwraps the result, making test coverage especially important to validate behavior when the key exists or is missing.
extra.suggested_action: Add test cases for `currentUserName()` covering both successful retrieval and missing username scenarios.

extra.title: New method `subscribeToUpdates()` lacks test coverage
extra.body: The new method `subscribeToUpdates()` introduces logic that subscribes to a publisher and calls `handleEvent(event)` for each received event. No test in this diff verifies that this subscription is established, that events are correctly handled via `handleEvent`, or that cancellables are properly stored.
extra.suggested_action: Add a test that publishes an event and verifies `handleEvent` is invoked with the correct event parameter.
